notes: 2.15M total games. 12 experiments across bid/reward/training/league all failed to break 80%. ARCHITECTURAL CHANGE REQUIRED. Reduces first layer from 8.3M to ~1M params, letting remaining capacity focus on strategy. (2) Double hidden size 512→1024. (3) Add attention over territories. Recommend option 1 first — simpler and addresses the root cause (signal buried in noise).

hypothesis: Float16 autocast + torch.compile should speed up V3 GNN training on M4 Max by reducing memory bandwidth for matmuls
notes: FAILED. torch.compile + autocast crashes MPS (type mismatch in compiled Metal graph). Autocast alone causes NaN explosion in GNN attention (float16 can't handle -inf masking + softmax). Even before NaN, was slower (404 sps vs 490 sps) — 690k param model is too small for mixed precision to help. Type conversion overhead dominates savings. Reverted to clean float32.

hypothesis: 5 architecture optimizations should dramatically speed up V3: (1) sparse message passing instead of dense O(T²) attention, (2) 2 GNN layers instead of 3, (3) embed dim 64 instead of 128, (4) remove dead W_msg weight, (5) pre-computed normalized adjacency
variable: GNN architecture (dense attention → sparse message passing, 3→2 layers, 128→64 embed)
old_value: Dense attention GNN, 3 layers, 128 embed, 690k params, 500 sps at 64 envs
new_value: Sparse message-passing GNN, 2 layers, 64 embed, 117k params, 2614 sps at 256 envs
notes: 5.2x speedup (500→2614 sps). GPU dropped from 80% to 57% — bottleneck shifted to Rust engine (36%). Bumped to 256 envs. 117k params (83% reduction from 690k). Adjacency is 97% sparse (5 neighbors avg per territory) — old GNN was computing 32x wasted attention scores. Need 50+ iterations to judge learning quality.

hypothesis: Monitoring: New sparse GNN V3 (117k params, 2 layers, 64 embed) training at 2605 sps with 256 envs. GPU 53%, Rust 39%. Allied 77% at 4.6k games — too early to judge (need 50+ iters).

hypothesis: Async pipeline (PPO in background thread + vectorized league + removed sync) should overlap GPU PPO training with Rust env stepping for higher throughput
variable: Training pipeline (sync → async PPO thread + vectorized league assignment)
old_value: Sequential: rollout then PPO, Python for-loop league, explicit mps.synchronize(). 2600 sps, GPU 55%, Rust 38%
new_value: Async: PPO in daemon thread overlaps next rollout, vectorized league with torch ops, no explicit sync. 3020 sps, GPU 72%, Rust 19%
notes: 16% speedup (2600→3020 sps). GPU now 72% utilized (was 55%). Rust dropped to 19% because it overlaps with PPO. Both PyTorch GPU ops and Rust/pyo3 release the GIL, enabling true thread parallelism on MPS. Allied 67% in sweet spot. Cumulative speedup from V3 dense: 500 → 3020 sps = 6x.
